utterances.text: Welcome to your onboarding kick-off. Today we want to align on milestones for the next thirty, sixty, and ninety days so the rollout is smooth.
Sounds good. Just to set context: we have forty reps across two regions and we need to move quickly because our existing tool contract ends in May.
Our biggest worry is data migration. We have about three years of historical sequence and call data we don't want to lose.
Understood. The good news is we have a documented migration template for your incumbent. We'll bring your sequence templates, prospect lists, and call history in.
Excellent. What about user permissions? Today we have very granular role-based access that took us a year to design.
Our role and permission model can mirror what you have. We will sit down with Petra in week two and configure roles, profiles, and team hierarchies before we onboard reps.
Sounds doable. One more concern: SSO. We are on Okta and we had a painful experience setting up SAML with our previous tool.
Okta is well supported. We have a step-by-step guide and most customers go live with SSO in about forty minutes once the IdP metadata is exchanged.
Great. Let's talk about training. What does the rep enablement look like?
We offer a three-session live track for reps, a manager track for your team leads, and an admin track for Petra and one backup admin. All recorded for new hires later.
Perfect. Can we get the admin track first so we are ready when reps come on board?
Yes, admin track runs in week one and two. Manager and rep tracks then run in weeks three to five.
Last thing from me: how do we measure success at the end of ninety days? We need clear numbers we can show our exec sponsor.
Three primary metrics: time to first sequence send per rep, number of active sequences per rep, and meetings booked per week. I'll send a template scorecard tomorrow.
Wonderful. I think we have everything we need to kick off. Thanks Naomi.
Thanks both, talk Friday at the first checkpoint.
